description: Upper part of a letter from Shelomo b. Yehuda, in Jerusalem, to the elders of the Palestinian congregation of Alexandria, specifically to the cantor Shela b. Moshe. Dating: Gil and Frenkel suggest 1025 CE, however this seems inconsistent with the events mentioned; late 1030s or 1040s seems more plausible. The letter deals with a prior complaint from the congregation in Alexandria against Yeshuʿa ha-Kohen he-Ḥaver b. Yosef and reprimands him ("if what you have said is true") for degrading the title of ḥaver. Shelomo b. Yehuda notes that Yeshuʿa should be grateful for achieving "that which neither his father (Yosef) nor his grandfather (Yeshuʿa) achieved," i.e., the title of ḥaver. He offers the addressees the choice of whether they wish to continue with Yeshuʿa as their communal leader or not. He then rejects the recommendation from Sahlān b. Avraham that "the cantor" (likely referring to Shela b. Moshe) be granted the title of ḥaver, apparently due to deficits in his own character. Related to T-S 12.328 (PGPID 1650). (Information in part from Frenkel and from Goitein, Med. Soc. 2:56 and 2:213–14.)
description_he: חלק עליון של מכתב משלמה בן יהודה, ירושלים, לזקני הקהילה הארץ ישראלית של אלכסנדריה. מענה לתלונה על ישועה הכהן החבר.
